analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (counterparty IDs, days 1–3): aligned each trade to last BBO/mid at ts; forward K∈{5,20,100} **price rows** (strictly future timestamps) for same-symbol mid, plus parallel extract/hydro markouts. Aggressor from price vs bid1/ask1. Burst ≥4 prints same (day,ts). Graph buyer→seller counts. Stratification: joint Sonic gate (5200&5300 spr≤2), symbol spread bins, hour in wide CSV.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate object below documents bullets 1–5; do not start Phase 2 until reviewed.

bullets.name: 1_participant_predictiveness
bullets.conclusion: Strongest same-tick party effects are **buy-aggressive** extract prints: **Mark 22 as seller** mean mark_20 +2.02 (n=90); **Mark 49 seller** +1.83 (n=104); **Mark 67 buyer** +1.81 (n=164); **Mark 14 seller** +0.58 (n=331). Many VEV cells near zero (penny strikes). Horizons K=5/20/100 broadly consistent for Mark67→Mark49 on extract.

bullets.name: 2_deviation_bot_baseline
bullets.conclusion: Cell-mean baseline vs global mean: largest |residual| pairs include **Mark 67→Mark 49** and **Mark 67→Mark 22** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** (mean mark_20 ≈ +1.63 to +2.03, n≈75–89). **Mark 38→Mark 14** hydro shows negative mean mark_20 (~−0.35, n=507). Residuals are interpretable as **scheduled bot legs**, not human IDs.

bullets.name: 3_sequence_graph
bullets.conclusion: Dominant edges: **Mark 01→Mark 22** (basket), **Mark 14↔Mark 38** (hydro duopoly loops), **Mark 55→Mark 01→Mark 22** two-hop volume. Static motif ranking in script log; no new dynamic chain beyond initial round4work summary.

bullets.name: 4_burst_structure
bullets.conclusion: n=293 bursts (≥4 prints); **mean extract mark_20 after burst = +0.33** vs **random control +0.15** — bursts associate with modestly higher forward extract mid path (event-study style, not causal).

bullets.name: 5_adverse_selection
bullets.conclusion: Conditional on **buy_aggr**, sellers with worst mean mark_20 on same symbol include **Mark 22 / Mark 49** on extract (passive seller adverse selection vs aggressive buyer), consistent with informed-style pressure on the offer.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: After **Mark 67 buy-aggressive** print on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** vs **Mark 49** (seller), forward **K=20** mid drift **positive** (~+1.8; n≈104; days 1–3 stable ~1.63/day).
**Mark 67→Mark 22** on extract, buy-aggr: mean mark_20 **+2.03** pooled (n=75); day stability mixed (day2 lower).
**Mark 14 as seller** on buy-aggr extract: mean mark_20 **+0.58** (n=331) — weaker but high-n.
**Multi-print bursts** (≥4 same ts): extract mark_20 **+0.33** vs random **+0.15** — structural scheduler signal.
**Mark 38→Mark 14** on **HYDROGEL_PACK**: negative forward same-symbol mark_20 (~−0.35, n=507) — fade/avoid passive offer into that leg if sim confirms.

no_edge_findings: **Mark 01→Mark 22** on VEV_5300/5400/5500: mean mark_20 near zero and **sign flips by day** (day1 negative, day2 positive) — **not** a stable directional edge at K=20.

**Joint Sonic gate TRUE** + traded symbol tight bin: **VEV_5300** mean mark_20 slightly **negative** (n=163) — gate alone does not clean voucher markouts on this tape without pairing with counterparty.
**Mark 55↔Mark 01** on extract: mean mark_20 ≈ 0 — no short-horizon predictability in cell means.
round4_phase1_complete.notes: All effects are **mid** markouts from public prices, not sim PnL. Phase 2 may add worse-fill backtests once a trader encodes these rules.

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 2: burst-window conditioning (±500 ts around Mark01→Mark22 n≥4 bursts), microprice deviation summary, extract spread-change vs 20-tick realized |Δmid|, contemporaneous extract vs VEV_5300 / hydro mid **return** correlations at lags, IV proxy residual (5200 vs extract) vs active Mark count, adverse extract table for sellers 22/49.
used_to_decide: See round4_phase2_complete gate for ranked edges and Phase-1 interaction.

bullets.name: 1_named_bot_burst
bullets.conclusion: Within ±500 of **Mark01→Mark22** bursts, **VEV_5300** mean mark_20 **−0.13** (n=162) vs **+1.25** off-window (n=2 only — not comparable). **VELVET** trades in window mean mark_20 **−0.11** vs **+0.40** off-window — **refines Phase 1**: basket-burst **neighborhood** coincides with **worse** short-horizon extract markouts, opposite naive burst-follow trend.

bullets.name: 2_microstructure
bullets.conclusion: Microprice vs mid: small mean |dev| on liquid names (see CSV). Extract **spread tightening vs widening** quintiles show **~flat** subsequent 20-tick |Δmid| sums (~17.2) — **no** strong spread-compression→vol edge in this discretization.

bullets.name: 3_cross_lead_lag
bullets.conclusion: **Contemporaneous** corr(extract Δmid, VEV_5300 Δmid) **~0.62** all three days; **lag ±3 ≈ 0** — lead–lag alpha is **same-bar co-move**, not predictive offset. Hydro corr ~0 at lag 0.

bullets.name: 4_regime_splits
bullets.conclusion: Regime split **burst window** materially changes markouts (extract + vouchers); complements Phase 1 joint-gate CSV (already split tight/wide × symbol spread bin).

bullets.name: 5_vol_smile
bullets.conclusion: Crude **(C−intrinsic)/S** proxy for VEV_5200 vs extract: **|iv_res|** essentially flat vs number of active Marks at timestamp — **no** Mark-count IV stress signal in this proxy.

bullets.name: 6_execution_adverse
bullets.conclusion: Confirms Phase 1: buy-aggr extract vs **Mark 22 / Mark 49** as seller shows large positive forward same-symbol markouts (seller adverse to buyer aggression).

bullets.name: 7_inventory_mm
bullets.conclusion: Deferred to sim/trader: cumulative signed flow per Mark vs extract drift needs tick-state loop; not shipped as table this round.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Fade / avoid providing liquidity** in **±500 timestamp** window around **Mark01→Mark22** multi-VEV bursts on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** (mean mark_20 **−0.11** in-window vs **+0.40** out; n=159 vs 1222).
top_5_tradeable_edges.orthogonal_to_phase1: Phase 1 showed unconditional burst **+** extract forward; conditioning on **who** (M01→M22) **inverts** sign for co-located extract prints.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Co-move basket:** contemporaneous **extract** vs **VEV_5300** mid returns corr **~0.62** (days 1–3); use for **hedge ratio / beta** not directional lag.
**VEV_5300** in M01→M22 burst window: negative short-horizon mark_20 (**−0.13**, n=162) — candidate **fade wing** after basket choreography (needs sim).
**Re-affirm Phase 1** Mark67→Mark49 / Mark67→Mark22 extract markouts — Phase 2 did **not** falsify; burst-window analysis adds **when not** to lean long (near M01→M22).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Confirms + refines timing context for Phase-1 positive extract legs.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 38→Mark 14** hydro negative markout (Phase 1) unchanged; Phase 2 microprice table shows hydro/extract microstructure stats for future MM skew.

round4_phase2_complete.phase1_interaction_summary: Phase 2 **refines** Phase 1 burst finding: **unconditional** burst extract forward **+** becomes **negative** for prints **near Mark01→Mark22** basket bursts — counterparty / orchestration matters. Phase 1 **Mark67** extract edges **stand** (not contradicted). Joint-gate-only VEV weakness from Phase 1 **unchanged**; Phase 3 should cross gate × burst × pair.

bullets.name: 1_joint_gate_surface
bullets.conclusion: P(joint tight) rises day 1→3 (~0.14, 0.17, 0.46 on 10k-tick subsample). **corr(s5200,s5300)** ~0.18–0.27 by day. **corr(s5200,s_ext)** and **corr(s5300,s_ext)** near **zero** (|r| < 0.03 typical) — inclineGod-style spread co-move is **between** core strikes, not extract spread vs voucher spreads on this discretization.

bullets.name: 2_pair_markout_by_gate
bullets.conclusion: **Mark 67→Mark 49** on extract: gate **False** n=77 mean mark_20 **+1.72** vs gate **True** n=12 mean **+1.08** — **negative interaction** (tight surface does not amplify this leg on tape). **Mark 01→Mark 22** on extract: **n=0** (basket on vouchers).

bullets.name: 3_m01_m22_voucher_legs
bullets.conclusion: M01→M22 prints are almost entirely under **gate=True**; **VEV_5300** pooled mean mark_20 **−0.12** (n=132). **5400/5500** means small positive ~0.04–0.03 — gate split adds little because **n_off_gate ≈ 0**.

bullets.name: 4_stack_with_phase2
bullets.conclusion: Phase 2 burst window already flags **when** extract short-horizon markouts weaken near M01→M22 choreography; Phase 3 shows **joint tight** can **dilute** the strongest Mark67→Mark49 extract signal — three-way design is Tier-A but **sim** (trader_v1/v2 trials) favored **not** gating extract on tight alone.

top_tier_interactions: **Joint tight × Mark67→Mark49 extract**: mean mark_20 lower when tight (see r4_phase3_summary.txt) — contradicts naive 'hedgeable surface ⇒ stronger drift' for this pair.
**M01→M22 × VEV_5300 × gate**: negative short-horizon mark_20 under dominant gate=True mass — aligns Phase 2 fade wing idea.

analysis_description: Round 4 trader iteration (counterparty → sim): **trader_v1** Sonic-gated extract hurt worse/all vs v0. **trader_v2** encodes Phase 2 burst window via offline `r4_burst_events.csv` centers ±500; conservative quoting **VEV_5300** only in-window (global VEV shrink zeroed voucher PnL under worse). Widening extract in burst window **hurt** worse-fill sim vs v0 — left extract at v0 in-window. Phase 3 (joint tight × post **Mark 67→Mark 49** buy-aggr extract) sim tweaks **hurt** day-2 worse PnL in trials — **not** shipped in v2.
used_to_decide: Prefer v0 or v2 for worse-fill anchor; v1 gated extract rejected for this tape. Next: stateful burst detection from `state.market_trades` if offline centers drift across tapes, or targeted extract skip only when extract prints co-locate with burst ts.

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 **day-stability supplement**: per-day n/mean/std for headline counterparty slices from existing `r4_trade_markouts_wide.csv` (no tape re-read). Validates K=5/20/100 for Mark67→Mark49 buy-aggr extract; hydro Mark38→Mark14 negative mean all 3 days.
used_to_decide: Tightens Phase 1 narrative: **Mark 14 as seller** buy-aggr extract mean mark_20 **shrinks** day 1→3 (1.32 → 0.44 → 0.09) while staying positive — high-n but **unstable magnitude**. **Mark67→Mark49** stable ~1.63/day at K=20 (3/3 positive). **Mark67→Mark22** positive all days but **day 2** mean **1.19** vs **2.36/2.68** days 1/3. **M67→M49 K=100**: day 3 mean **+0.11** vs **+2.4/+3.0** days 1/2 — long-horizon sign weak on day 3.

analysis_description: **Backtest engine:** `TestRunner.__initialize_trade_state` now copies this tick’s public `Trade` rows into `state.market_trades` before `trader.run` (Round 4 buyer/seller visible at decision time). **Tape evidence:** `r4_burst_live_vs_offline_ticks.csv` counts timestamps where live burst (≥4 Mark01→Mark22 prints same ts) vs offline ±500 window from `r4_burst_events.csv` — offline dominates (813–1141 ticks/day) because choreography affects neighbors; live-only gap is **1 tick day1, 0 day2–3**. **trader_v3.py** uses live OR offline (same VEV_5300 nudge as v2); worse/all **46,795 / 49,855** match v2 on d1–3.
used_to_decide: Future counterparty-conditioned traders can read `state.market_trades` without forking data readers. For **burst neighborhood** (Phase 2), offline ±500 or rolling tape scan still needed — live same-ts burst is a **subset** of the Phase-2 window.

analysis_description: **Phase 1 hydro edge → sim:** Pooled **Mark 38→Mark 14** on **HYDROGEL_PACK** has negative mean mark_20_same (Phase 1); **joint_gate_tight** splits are **inconsistent by day** (see `r4_phase1_hydro_m38_m14_mark20_by_gate_day.csv` — e.g. day2 gate=True mean still negative). **trader_v4** implements **unconditional** post-print caution: any M38→M14 hydro print at current ts extends 400-tick wider-edge / half-size hydro quotes. Backtest R4 d1–3: **worse +488** vs v0 (47,283 vs 46,795), **all +488** vs v0 (50,528 vs 50,040).
used_to_decide: First counterparty rule that **improves** primary worse-fill total on this tape; do **not** stack naive joint-gate filter on this hydro pair without re-tabling by day.

analysis_description: **Stack test (Phase 2 on Phase 1 sim):** **trader_v5** = v4 hydro M38→M14 caution + M01→M22 burst VEV_5300 nudge (v3 logic). R4 d1–3: **worse 47,283** — **same** per-day totals as v4 (burst tweak net-neutral under worse). **All 50,343** vs v4 **50,528** (−185) — extra VEV_5300 conservatism hurts all-fill path without helping worse.
used_to_decide: Ship **v4** as best worse-fill combo so far; **v5** not an upgrade. Next stack candidate: Mark67→Mark49 **directional** extract (tape positive) vs sim adverse-selection risk — needs careful size.

analysis_description: **trader_v6** tests Phase-1 **Mark67→Mark49 buy-aggr extract** as a **+1** fair-anchor lift for **250** ticks after the print, gated **off** Sonic joint tight (Phase 3 interaction). Stacked on **v4** hydro rule. R4 d1–3: **worse 47,271** (−12 vs v4 47,283) — small **day-2** extract regression under worse. **All 51,920** (+1,392 vs v4) driven largely by **day2 extract** — confirms tape mid drift ≠ worse-fill extract PnL for this nudge.
used_to_decide: Do **not** ship v6 for worse-fill objective; keep **v4**. Optional: shrink bias window or bias strength; or only bias **bids** not asks if revisiting.

analysis_description: **trader_v7** (v4 + M67→M49 bid-only +1 tick on extract bid anchor for 250 ts, gate-off): **worse 47,083** (−200 vs v4 47,283). Per-product log: **day3 extract** 3,385 vs v4 **3,585** — the bid skew still worsened worse-fill extract on day3; days 1–2 extract unchanged vs v4. **All 50,196** (−332 vs v4) — bid-only does not replicate v6’s all uplift.
used_to_decide: Abandon symmetric and bid-only **fi** nudges for this tape under worse; **v4** remains best worse baseline. Any M67→M49 extract rule likely needs **different** action (e.g. skip sell only, or size cap) + longer sim grid.
